Class ReuseVsRecreateBenchmark

java.lang.Object
oshi.benchmark.ReuseVsRecreateBenchmark
Direct Known Subclasses:
ReuseVsRecreateBenchmark_jmhType_B1
public class ReuseVsRecreateBenchmark extends Object
Measures the CPU cost of reusing a SystemInfo (holding the constructed object graph) versus creating a new one for every poll, for the metrics a monitoring agent samples repeatedly (CPU ticks, available memory, process list).

The memoizer is disabled (oshi.util.memoizer.expiration = 0) in setup() because real monitoring polls (e.g. once per second) always fall outside the memoizer's sub-second TTL — so every poll is a cache miss, and the honest question is the cost of reconstructing the objects, not of a cache hit.

Pair this with -prof gc (allocation per poll) and MonitoringFootprintReport (retained memory of the held object graph) for the full CPU-vs-memory picture documented in PERFORMANCE.md. 

  java -jar oshi-benchmark/target/benchmarks.jar ReuseVsRecreateBenchmark -prof gc

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static void
    main(String[] args)
    Standalone entry point.
    Object
    recreate()
    Polls the metric from a freshly constructed SystemInfo, rebuilding the object graph on every poll.
    Object
    reuse()
    Polls the metric from a reused SystemInfo, so the constructed object graph is not rebuilt.
    void
    setup()
    Disables memoization (so each poll is a real query, as it would be at realistic poll intervals) and builds the reusable SystemInfo, realizing the subsystem under test.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • ReuseVsRecreateBenchmark

      public ReuseVsRecreateBenchmark()
      Creates a new benchmark instance. Required by JMH for @State classes.

  • Method Details

    • setup

      public void setup()
      Disables memoization (so each poll is a real query, as it would be at realistic poll intervals) and builds the reusable SystemInfo, realizing the subsystem under test.

    • reuse

      public Object reuse()
      Polls the metric from a reused SystemInfo, so the constructed object graph is not rebuilt.
      Returns:
      the metric result

    • recreate

      public Object recreate()
      Polls the metric from a freshly constructed SystemInfo, rebuilding the object graph on every poll.
      Returns:
      the metric result

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args) throws org.openjdk.jmh.runner.RunnerException
      Standalone entry point.
      Parameters:
      args - unused
      Throws:
      org.openjdk.jmh.runner.RunnerException - if the benchmark fails to run
